findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated styling/branding text and a Google Fonts URL that do not appear in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML head contains text such as "Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS", CSS variables like "--nc-navy", "--nc-blue", and an import URL "https://fonts.googleapis.com/...". The PDF pages only show the report title "FNS NEGATIVE CASE PAYMENT ACCURACY RATES" with the table; no branding/CSS text or URLs are visible in the source.

findings.description: Table data integrity issue in the STATE AGENCY row: the accuracy rate value is missing in HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF bottom summary row shows "STATE AGENCY 91 91 22.14%" with 22.14% under ACCURACY RATE and the ERROR RATE cell blank. HTML row is rendered as "STATE AGENCY919122.14%", placing 22.14% under ERROR RATE and leaving ACCURACY RATE blank.

findings.description: HTML structure contains invalid/nested main landmarks and uses only a single h1 while the PDF is a simple title-plus-table layout; the structural markup does not cleanly reflect the document organization.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains "
...
...

FNS NEGATIVE CASE PAYMENT ACCURACY RATES

...
..." with a nested
. The PDF shows one report title and then the table across pages, without a nested section structure.
